metrics.name: Typical Case Timeline (County)
metrics.description: Median and P90 days from filing to final judgment for your case type in your jurisdiction.

specification.definition: Median (P50) and P90 elapsed calendar days from case filing to final judgment or dismissal, stratified by case type (dissolution with children, paternity, modification, custody) and judicial circuit.
specification.numerator: Sum of elapsed days across all qualifying cases
specification.denominator: Count of qualifying dispositions in period (median) or distribution percentile (P90)
specification.source: Court case management system (Missouri OSCA for pilot)
specification.inclusion: Cases with a recorded filing date and a recorded disposition date in the reporting window
specification.exclusion: Sealed cases, transferred cases (counted in destination circuit), cases with disposition type "administrative closure" without merits ruling
specification.confounders: Case complexity, party cooperation, attorney availability, pandemic-era backlogs, assignment court differences
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level publication requires n ≥ 25 dispositions of the case type per reporting period
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Sets realistic expectations for pro se parents. Enables them to plan financially and emotionally. Surfaces disparities between circuits that invite examination.

metrics.name: Custody Outcome Distribution
metrics.description: How often courts in your county award sole vs joint physical and legal custody.

specification.definition: Proportion of dissolutions and paternity cases with minor children that result in sole vs joint legal custody, and sole vs joint physical custody, at final judgment.
specification.numerator: Count of cases with the specified custody outcome
specification.denominator: Total cases with a custody determination in the reporting period
specification.source: Court case management system, custody determination codes
specification.inclusion: Cases with minor children and a final custody order in the reporting year
specification.exclusion: Cases where custody was not in dispute, cases transferred to juvenile court, protective-order-only custody matters
specification.confounders: Child age distribution, distance between parents' residences, pre-existing parenting plans, prior protective orders
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level publication requires n ≥ 30 cases per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Pro se parents currently rely on anecdote. Publishing actual distributions corrects misperceptions in both directions and supports realistic settlement discussions.

metrics.name: Parenting Time Compliance Rate
metrics.description: Percentage of court-ordered parenting time actually exercised. Requires user-logged data.

specification.definition: Ratio of actual parenting time hours exercised to the parenting time hours scheduled under the current court order, measured per calendar month per parent.
specification.numerator: Hours of parenting time exercised (logged exchanges plus documented custody periods)
specification.denominator: Hours of parenting time scheduled under active custody order
specification.source: User-logged exchange data from CoTrackPro or equivalent platform, cross-referenced with court order of record
specification.inclusion: Active cases with a final parenting plan in force for at least 90 days, both parents participating in logging
specification.exclusion: Cases under emergency modification, cases with active no-contact provisions, cases with documented mutual deviation agreements
specification.confounders: Work schedule changes, child illness, mutual voluntary deviations, holidays, child preferences as they mature, travel
specification.min_sample: No publication for cells with n < 10 cases. Differential privacy noise applied to cells with n < 30
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Currently invisible. Publication surfaces enforcement gaps and reveals whether court orders match lived reality. Foundational for any reform of enforcement systems.

metrics.name: Child Contact Gap
metrics.description: Consecutive days a child goes without contact with either parent, by case type.

specification.definition: Longest consecutive period in days during which a child has no contact (in-person, phone, or video) with either parent, measured rolling over the prior 90 days.
specification.numerator: Maximum gap in days between documented contact events
specification.denominator: Not a rate — a distribution metric reported as P50 and P90
specification.source: User-logged contact events, exchange logs, documented calls and video sessions
specification.inclusion: Active cases with minor children, court order in force, both parents with some form of ordered contact
specification.exclusion: Cases where contact is legally suspended, child has aged out (18+), documented periods of medical isolation
specification.confounders: Travel, illness, mutual family decisions, child autonomous choices as they age, incarceration
specification.min_sample: n ≥ 10 per reporting cell required for publication
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Captures human harm of enforcement failures invisible to case-flow metrics. Emotionally resonant without being sensationalized.

metrics.name: Motion Grant Rates (County)
metrics.description: How often emergency, contempt, and modification motions are granted — aggregated, anonymized.

specification.definition: Proportion of filed motions of each type (emergency, contempt, modification, relocation) that result in a grant ruling, stratified by circuit.
specification.numerator: Motions granted in full or in part during the reporting period
specification.denominator: Total motions of that type resolved (granted, denied, or dismissed) in the reporting period
specification.source: Court case management system, motion disposition codes
specification.inclusion: All motions of the specified type with a recorded disposition
specification.exclusion: Motions withdrawn by the moving party before ruling, motions mooted by settlement
specification.confounders: Motion quality, self-represented vs attorney-filed, underlying case merit, local procedural rules
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 30 motions of the type per year
specification.privacy: De-identified. Access gated by DUA with IRB approval required. Re-identification attempts prohibited with permanent revocation as penalty. Audit log of every query.
specification.reform_value: Lets parents calibrate expectations before filing. Reduces speculative motions that burden the docket. Surfaces unusual patterns inviting administrative review.

metrics.name: Time-to-Enforcement
metrics.description: From violation filed to enforcement action. Defensibly publishable at aggregate level.

specification.definition: Elapsed calendar days from the filing of an enforcement motion (typically contempt or motion to enforce parenting time) to the first substantive enforcement action by the court.
specification.numerator: Sum of elapsed days across qualifying motions
specification.denominator: Count of qualifying enforcement motions with action taken
specification.source: Court case management system, motion filing and action timestamps
specification.inclusion: Enforcement motions filed during the reporting window that received any court action (hearing scheduled, order entered, dismissal with reasons)
specification.exclusion: Motions withdrawn before action, motions mooted by voluntary compliance, motions in cases under emergency stay
specification.confounders: Backlog, motion complexity, service-of-process delays, consolidation with other pending matters
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 20 enforcement motions per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Enforcement delay is the gap where parenting plans silently fail. Measuring it is the first step to closing it.

metrics.name: Represented vs Pro Se Outcomes
metrics.description: Outcome differentials by representation status, controlled for case complexity.

specification.definition: Difference in key outcomes (custody distribution, child support guideline adherence, motion grant rates) between cases where one or both parties are represented and cases where neither is, controlling for case complexity via matched-case analysis.
specification.numerator: Outcome variable means for each representation class
specification.denominator: Count of cases in each class
specification.period: Annual analysis with 12-month lag for appellate settlement
specification.source: Court case management system, party representation records, case outcome data
specification.inclusion: Dissolution, paternity, modification, and contested custody cases with final disposition in the reporting year
specification.exclusion: Uncontested matters, default judgments, cases with representation changes mid-proceeding
specification.confounders: Self-selection into representation (cases with attorneys may be more complex), socioeconomic status, case type mix, judicial assignment
specification.min_sample: Publication requires n ≥ 100 in each representation class per circuit per year
specification.privacy: De-identified. Access gated by DUA with IRB approval required. Re-identification attempts prohibited with permanent revocation as penalty. Audit log of every query.
specification.reform_value: Foundational disparity metric. Informs debate about court-appointed representation in family matters and pro se accessibility reforms.

metrics.name: Peer Case Duration Benchmark
metrics.description: Anonymized case duration distribution for similar matter types, controlling for complexity.

specification.definition: Distribution of case duration in days across matched attorneys for each matter type, with controls for case complexity factors (number of issues contested, contested custody, business valuation, protective order presence).
specification.numerator: Elapsed days per attorney-case pairing
specification.denominator: Count of attorney-case pairings in the complexity cohort
specification.source: Court case management system with attorney-of-record linkage, case characteristic coding
specification.inclusion: Attorneys with at least 15 dispositions in the window and matter type, cases with full characteristic coding
specification.exclusion: Pro se cases, limited-scope representations, cases with representation changes
specification.confounders: Client cooperation, opposing counsel tactics, case law evolution, local rule differences
specification.min_sample: Benchmark returned only to attorneys with n ≥ 15 cases in the window
specification.privacy: De-identified. Access gated by DUA with IRB approval required. Re-identification attempts prohibited with permanent revocation as penalty. Audit log of every query. Attorneys see only their own percentile, never others' individual data.
specification.reform_value: Professional self-calibration tool. Reduces unnecessary litigation and improves client service without exposing individual attorneys to public comparison.

metrics.name: Motion Success Rate by Type
metrics.description: Grant rates for each motion type in the jurisdiction, across all attorneys.

specification.definition: Jurisdiction-wide grant rate for each motion type, providing baseline context for attorneys evaluating their own performance.
specification.numerator: Motions granted in full or in part
specification.denominator: Total motions resolved of that type
specification.source: Court case management system, motion disposition codes
specification.inclusion: All motions of the specified type with recorded disposition
specification.exclusion: Withdrawn motions, mooted motions, emergency ex parte only (counted separately)
specification.confounders: Local rules, judicial assignment, underlying case merit distribution
specification.min_sample: Jurisdiction-level requires n ≥ 30 motions of type per year
specification.privacy: De-identified. Access gated by DUA with IRB approval required. Re-identification attempts prohibited with permanent revocation as penalty. Audit log of every query.
specification.reform_value: Informs strategy and client counseling. Reduces low-merit motions filed without context.

metrics.name: Continuance Frequency
metrics.description: Average continuances per case type per jurisdiction.

specification.definition: Mean and median number of continuances granted per case from filing to disposition, stratified by case type and circuit.
specification.numerator: Total continuances granted across cases
specification.denominator: Total cases in the reporting period
specification.source: Court case management system, continuance records
specification.inclusion: Cases with at least one scheduled hearing in the window
specification.exclusion: Administrative continuances (court schedule changes), cases with no hearings scheduled
specification.confounders: Court calendar pressure, judicial leave, weather events, attorney scheduling conflicts
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 50 cases per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Operational metric revealing docket friction. Trend analysis informs resource allocation.

metrics.name: GAL Recommendation Alignment
metrics.description: How often final rulings match GAL recommendations in your jurisdiction.

specification.definition: Proportion of cases with a GAL appointment where the final ruling substantially matches the GAL's written recommendation, aggregated by circuit.
specification.numerator: Cases with substantial ruling-to-recommendation alignment
specification.denominator: Cases with a GAL recommendation and a final ruling
specification.source: Court case management system, GAL reports, final judgments
specification.inclusion: Cases with formal GAL appointment, written recommendation filed, final judgment entered
specification.exclusion: Cases settled before GAL recommendation, cases with multiple sequential GAL appointments (counted once)
specification.confounders: Settlement patterns (cases closest to GAL recommendation may settle; rulings are on hardest cases), GAL scope of appointment differences
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 20 GAL-recommended cases per period
specification.privacy: De-identified. Access gated by DUA with IRB approval required. Re-identification attempts prohibited with permanent revocation as penalty. Audit log of every query.
specification.reform_value: Calibrates attorney expectations about GAL influence. Detects unusual patterns warranting administrative review.

metrics.name: Settlement vs Trial Distribution
metrics.description: Proportion of cases settled vs litigated, by case type.

specification.definition: Proportion of filed family court cases that reach final resolution through settlement (with or without mediation) versus contested trial, by case type and circuit.
specification.numerator: Cases in each resolution class
specification.denominator: Total resolved cases in the period
specification.source: Court case management system, disposition type codes
specification.inclusion: Cases with final disposition in the reporting year
specification.exclusion: Cases transferred before disposition, administrative dismissals
specification.confounders: Local practice norms, mediation program availability, case complexity mix
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 50 cases per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Baseline operational metric. Informs mediation investment and settlement program design.

metrics.name: Appeal Reversal Rate
metrics.description: Proportion of family court rulings reversed on appeal, by issue type.

specification.definition: Proportion of appellate dispositions of family court rulings that reverse, modify, or remand the lower court decision, stratified by issue type (custody, support, property division, contempt).
specification.numerator: Appellate dispositions with reversal, modification, or remand outcomes
specification.denominator: Total appellate dispositions of family court rulings
specification.source: Appellate court case management systems, cross-referenced with lower court case identifiers
specification.inclusion: Appeals of family court rulings with final appellate disposition
specification.exclusion: Appeals dismissed on procedural grounds, appeals withdrawn before ruling
specification.confounders: Selection bias (only appealed cases appear), appellate panel assignment
specification.min_sample: Statewide requires n ≥ 30 appellate dispositions per issue type per period
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Identifies issue types where lower court rulings systematically diverge from appellate standards.

metrics.name: Personal Docket Pace
metrics.description: Your time-to-disposition vs bench peers, controlled for case mix. Private to you.

specification.definition: Individual judge's median and P90 time-to-disposition compared to a peer benchmark of same-bench judges, controlling for case type and complexity mix.
specification.numerator: Judge's elapsed-day values across qualifying cases
specification.denominator: Case count in the judge's docket for the period
specification.source: Court case management system with judge-of-record assignment
specification.inclusion: Cases assigned to the judge for at least 80% of their active life, final disposition in window
specification.exclusion: Cases transferred in or out mid-proceeding, cases under emergency stay
specification.confounders: Case complexity differences not fully captured by coding, docket size variance, judicial leave
specification.min_sample: Individual benchmark requires n ≥ 50 dispositions in the window
specification.privacy: Tier B private. Returned only to the individual judge and the presiding judge of the circuit. Never aggregated with judge identifiers for public release.
specification.reform_value: Supports judicial self-improvement without exposing individual judges to comparison pressure from outside the bench.

metrics.name: Decision Consistency Score
metrics.description: Variance in your rulings on similar fact patterns over time. Private self-assessment tool.

specification.definition: Within-judge variance in rulings on cases with substantially similar fact patterns, measured over a rolling 24-month window using matched case analysis.
specification.numerator: Standard deviation of rulings on matched case pairs
specification.source: Court case management system, de-identified case abstracts, matched using propensity score or exact matching
specification.inclusion: Judges with at least 50 family court dispositions in the window
specification.exclusion: Bench trials with sealed findings, cases transferred mid-proceeding
specification.confounders: Case complexity differences not captured in match, evolving case law, statutory changes within window
specification.min_sample: Score calculated only for judges with n ≥ 50 dispositions
specification.privacy: Tier B — private to the individual judge only. Never aggregated publicly with judge identifiers. No external disclosure.
specification.reform_value: Professional self-improvement tool. Detects drift. Never used for ranking or discipline.

metrics.name: Reversal Rate
metrics.description: Your appellate reversal rate by issue type, benchmarked against circuit average.

specification.definition: Individual judge's appellate reversal rate by issue type, with circuit and state peer benchmarks.
specification.numerator: Judge's rulings reversed, modified, or remanded on appeal
specification.denominator: Judge's rulings that were appealed and received appellate disposition
specification.source: Appellate court records cross-referenced with lower court judge assignments
specification.inclusion: Appealed rulings with final appellate disposition
specification.exclusion: Procedural dismissals, withdrawn appeals
specification.confounders: Selection bias, appellate panel variance, case complexity in appealed subset
specification.min_sample: Individual score requires n ≥ 20 appealed rulings
specification.privacy: Tier B private. Shared with judge, presiding judge, and judicial education programs only.
specification.reform_value: Supports targeted continuing judicial education. Identifies areas where bench-wide training may improve consistency.

metrics.name: Guideline Deviation Rate
metrics.description: Your child support deviation rate and direction, relative to state median.

specification.definition: Proportion of child support orders entered by the judge that deviate from presumed guideline amounts, and the direction and magnitude of deviation.
specification.numerator: Support orders with documented deviation
specification.source: Child support order records with deviation findings
specification.inclusion: Final support orders with recorded guideline calculation
specification.exclusion: Temporary orders, stipulated orders entered without hearing
specification.confounders: Case demographics, cost-of-living variance within circuit, specific deviation factors (medical, childcare, travel)
specification.min_sample: Individual rate requires n ≥ 30 support orders entered
specification.privacy: Tier B private. Benchmark returned to judge and presiding judge.
specification.reform_value: Supports consistency in child support application. Identifies potential need for guideline revision if systematic deviation patterns emerge statewide.

metrics.name: Courtroom Time Allocation
metrics.description: Time granted per case by party and issue. Detects imbalance.

specification.definition: Distribution of courtroom time allocated to each party and to each issue during hearings and trials, measured by party and by issue category.
specification.numerator: Minutes of speaking time per party/issue
specification.source: Court recordings with time-coded transcripts (requires transcription infrastructure)
specification.inclusion: Hearings and trials with full transcription available
specification.exclusion: Ex parte proceedings, in-chambers conferences
specification.confounders: Issue complexity differences, counsel preparation, court timekeeping accuracy
specification.min_sample: Individual judge analysis requires n ≥ 30 proceedings
specification.privacy: Tier B private. Individual judge and presiding judge only.
specification.reform_value: Detects systematic imbalance without requiring subjective assessment. Supports fair-hearing integrity.

metrics.name: Disposition Backlog
metrics.description: Open matters on your docket beyond court-set disposition targets.

specification.definition: Count and proportion of cases open beyond court-set age-of-case disposition targets (e.g., 12-month standard for dissolutions), by judge and aggregated to circuit.
specification.numerator: Open cases older than disposition target
specification.source: Court case management system, case age calculations
specification.inclusion: Cases assigned to the judge at the snapshot date
specification.exclusion: Cases under emergency stay, cases awaiting appellate remand
specification.confounders: Docket size, case-type mix, recent assignment changes, judicial leave
specification.min_sample: Publicly published only at circuit level; individual judge data internal
specification.privacy: Circuit-aggregate public (Tier A). Individual-judge internal (Tier B).
specification.reform_value: Operational management tool. Transparency at circuit level supports resource-allocation discussions.

metrics.name: Docket-Level Backlog
metrics.description: Open cases past disposition targets, by division and case type.

specification.definition: Circuit-level count and proportion of open cases past age-of-case targets, stratified by case type and division.
specification.numerator: Open cases older than target age
specification.source: Court case management system
specification.inclusion: All open cases at snapshot date
specification.exclusion: Cases under appellate stay or formal abatement
specification.confounders: Filing surges, judicial vacancies, holiday schedules
specification.min_sample: Publishable at any circuit with n ≥ 20 cases in division
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Standard operational metric. Supports resource-allocation decisions at circuit and state level.

metrics.name: Clearance Rate
metrics.description: Cases resolved divided by cases filed, rolling quarterly.

specification.definition: Ratio of cases resolved to cases filed in the period, by case type and circuit. A value of 1.0 means the circuit is keeping pace with filings; below 1.0 indicates growing backlog.
specification.numerator: Cases resolved in period
specification.source: Court case management system
specification.inclusion: All filings and dispositions in the period
specification.exclusion: Administrative reclassifications
specification.confounders: Seasonal filing patterns, case-type mix changes
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level with n ≥ 20 per case type
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: The canonical workload metric for court administration.

metrics.name: Mediation Diversion Rate
metrics.description: Proportion of cases that reach settlement via court-referred mediation.

specification.definition: Proportion of cases referred to court-affiliated mediation programs that reach settlement (partial or full) without further litigation.
specification.numerator: Mediation-referred cases reaching settlement
specification.source: Court case management system, mediation program records
specification.inclusion: Cases with a formal referral to court-affiliated mediation program
specification.exclusion: Private mediations not court-referred, mediations in cases with subsequent emergency filings
specification.confounders: Case complexity, mediator skill variance, client motivation
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 30 referrals per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Quantifies value of mediation programs. Supports budget justification and program expansion decisions.

metrics.name: Emergency Motion Load
metrics.description: Volume and trend of ex parte and emergency filings.

specification.definition: Count and rate of ex parte and emergency motions filed per 100 active cases, tracked monthly.
specification.numerator: Emergency and ex parte motions filed
specification.source: Court case management system, motion coding
specification.inclusion: Motions coded as emergency or ex parte
specification.exclusion: Motions re-coded after initial filing
specification.confounders: Coding practice differences across circuits, pandemic and disaster surge effects
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires monthly case load ≥ 100
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Operational load indicator. Trend spikes invite inquiry into underlying conflict drivers.

metrics.name: Re-filing Rate
metrics.description: Same-parties returning within 12/24/36 months. A reform priority metric.

specification.definition: Proportion of closed cases where the same parties return to court on a new matter within 12, 24, and 36 months of the prior disposition.
specification.numerator: Closed cases where same parties re-filed within the window
specification.denominator: Total closed cases in the baseline year
specification.source: Court case management system with party-matching algorithm
specification.inclusion: Cases with minor children or ongoing child support obligations
specification.exclusion: Parties with name changes not detected by matching algorithm
specification.confounders: Party-matching algorithm accuracy, cases transferred to other jurisdictions
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires baseline cohort of n ≥ 100
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Measures the durability of court orders. High re-filing rates indicate rulings that do not resolve the underlying dispute and invite structural review.

metrics.name: Self-Represented Litigant Share
metrics.description: Proportion of filings with no counsel, by case type. Informs resource planning.

specification.definition: Proportion of case filings where at least one party is self-represented, stratified by case type, at filing and at final disposition.
specification.numerator: Cases with at least one pro se party
specification.source: Court case management system, representation records
specification.inclusion: All family court filings
specification.exclusion: Cases where representation status is not recorded
specification.confounders: Representation changes mid-case, limited-scope representations
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 100 cases per type
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Foundational metric for self-help center resource allocation, clerk training, and procedural simplification priorities.

metrics.name: Caseload Distribution
metrics.description: Active cases per GAL in your jurisdiction, and concentration among repeat appointees.

specification.definition: Distribution of active case appointments across individual GALs in a circuit, including Herfindahl-Hirschman-style concentration measure for appointment distribution.
specification.numerator: Active appointments per GAL
specification.denominator: Total active GAL appointments in circuit
specification.source: Court case management system, GAL appointment records
specification.inclusion: Active GAL appointments at snapshot date
specification.exclusion: GAL appointments in closed cases
specification.confounders: GAL experience and capacity differences, specialty case types
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 10 active GALs
specification.privacy: Tier B with DUA. Circuit-level aggregate public; individual GAL data restricted to administrative review.
specification.reform_value: Detects over-concentration of appointments among few GALs, which may indicate informal selection patterns.

metrics.name: Recommendation-to-Ruling Alignment
metrics.description: Proportion of final rulings that match GAL recommendations.

specification.definition: Proportion of cases where the court's final ruling substantially matches the GAL's recommendation, aggregated by circuit.
specification.numerator: Cases with substantial alignment
specification.denominator: Cases with a GAL recommendation and final ruling
specification.source: GAL reports and final judgments, coded for alignment by trained coders
specification.inclusion: Cases with written GAL recommendation and final ruling
specification.exclusion: Cases settled before recommendation, GAL withdrawals
specification.confounders: Settlement patterns, scope of GAL appointment
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 20
specification.privacy: Tier B. Aggregate-circuit data publishable; individual GAL data restricted.
specification.reform_value: Supports understanding of GAL influence on case outcomes and identifies potential training needs.

metrics.name: Time-to-Report
metrics.description: Days from appointment to filed recommendation, benchmarked.

specification.definition: Elapsed days from GAL appointment order to the filing of the written recommendation.
specification.numerator: Sum of elapsed days across GAL reports
specification.source: Court case management system, GAL appointment and filing records
specification.inclusion: GAL appointments with recommendation filed in period
specification.exclusion: Appointments withdrawn before report filed, extensions granted for good cause
specification.confounders: Case complexity, child availability for interviews, GAL caseload
specification.min_sample: Individual GAL requires n ≥ 10 reports
specification.privacy: Tier B with DUA.
specification.reform_value: Measures investigative thoroughness and timeliness. Benchmarks support professional development.

metrics.name: Child Contact Frequency
metrics.description: Average number of child interviews per case. Measures investigative depth.

specification.definition: Mean number of documented in-person or video interviews with the subject child(ren) during the GAL's appointment.
specification.numerator: Total documented child interviews
specification.source: GAL report documentation
specification.inclusion: GAL cases with final recommendation filed
specification.exclusion: Cases with infants or children too young for interview
specification.confounders: Child age, child availability, case urgency, travel distance
specification.min_sample: Individual GAL requires n ≥ 10 cases
specification.privacy: Tier B with DUA.
specification.reform_value: Signals investigative engagement with the child, the party most affected.

metrics.name: Repeat Appointment Index
metrics.description: Concentration of appointments with specific judges — detects informal networks.

specification.definition: Proportion of a specific GAL's appointments that come from a single judge, aggregated to identify unusual patterns of repeat engagement.
specification.numerator: Appointments from a single judge
specification.source: Court appointment records
specification.inclusion: All formal GAL appointments
specification.exclusion: Emergency or stand-in appointments of less than 14 days
specification.confounders: Specialized case types, geographic accessibility, judge caseload differences
specification.min_sample: Individual GAL requires n ≥ 15 appointments
specification.privacy: Tier B. Publication at circuit-aggregate only.
specification.reform_value: Detects potential informal networks inviting administrative review. Supports fair rotation of appointments.

metrics.name: Statewide Case Volume Trend
metrics.description: Filings per 1,000 population, by case type, five-year trend.

specification.definition: Annual family court filings normalized per 1,000 state population, disaggregated by case type, with five-year trend analysis.
specification.numerator: Case filings of the type in the year
specification.source: Missouri OSCA annual statistics; U.S. Census Bureau population estimates
specification.inclusion: All family court filings statewide
specification.exclusion: Refiling of previously dismissed cases (counted once)
specification.confounders: Population trend, demographic shifts, filing fee changes, e-filing adoption
specification.min_sample: Always publishable at state level
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Identifies growth areas and informs capacity planning. Foundational metric for legislative appropriations.

metrics.name: Litigation-Years-Per-Child
metrics.description: Total court-exposed years for minor children statewide. A harm index.

specification.definition: Sum of (case duration in years × number of minor children per case), summed statewide and expressed per 1,000 minors.
specification.numerator: Total (case-years × children), summed across all qualifying cases
specification.source: State court case management data, Census minor population estimates
specification.inclusion: All cases involving minor children (dissolution with children, paternity, modification, custody, protective orders with custody implications)
specification.exclusion: Cases sealed under juvenile statutes, adult-only matters
specification.confounders: Multi-case families (same children in multiple cases), interstate custody transfers
specification.min_sample: State-level minimum; county-level requires n ≥ 25 cases per year
specification.privacy: Fully aggregate. No individual, family, or judge identifiers involved.
specification.reform_value: Single most powerful statistic for legislative reform framing — quantifies childhood years spent in litigation.

metrics.name: Financial Depletion Indicator
metrics.description: Bankruptcies filed during or within two years of family court proceedings.

specification.definition: Count and rate of personal bankruptcy filings involving at least one party with a concurrent or recent (within 24 months) family court case.
specification.numerator: Bankruptcy filings with matched family court involvement
specification.source: Federal bankruptcy court records cross-referenced with state family court records via party-matching
specification.inclusion: Personal bankruptcy filings (Chapter 7 and 13)
specification.exclusion: Business bankruptcy, matched records with uncertain identity match
specification.confounders: Party-matching accuracy, underlying financial distress independent of litigation
specification.min_sample: State-level publication
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. Matching performed in a one-way pseudonymized pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Quantifies economic harm of family court litigation. Informs debate about court cost-shifting and access reforms.

metrics.name: County-Level Disparity
metrics.description: Outcome variance across counties controlling for demographics. Evidence for standardization.

specification.definition: Coefficient of variation of key outcome metrics (case duration, custody distribution, motion grant rates) across counties after controlling for demographic and case-type composition.
specification.numerator: Standard deviation of adjusted county outcomes
specification.source: Court case management system, Census demographic data
specification.inclusion: All counties with n ≥ 50 cases per year
specification.exclusion: Counties below minimum sample
specification.confounders: Unmeasured demographic variation, case mix differences not captured in coding
specification.min_sample: Statewide analysis requires counties covering 80% of state population
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Supports the case for statewide standardization when county variance exceeds what demographic differences explain.

metrics.name: DV Escalation Post-Filing
metrics.description: Protective order violations following custody filings. Safety signal.

specification.definition: Rate of protective order violations occurring within 180 days after a custody-related filing in the same party pairing.
specification.numerator: Protective order violations matched to recent custody filings
specification.denominator: Custody filings with a related protective order in force
specification.source: Court case management system cross-referenced with law enforcement violation records
specification.inclusion: Custody filings with party-linked protective orders
specification.exclusion: Violations in counties below minimum suppression threshold (see Protective Order Data Confidentiality)
specification.confounders: Reporting variance, party matching accuracy, underlying conflict intensity
specification.min_sample: Statewide aggregate only; never county-level without n ≥ 25 per reporting period
specification.privacy: Tier B. VAWA-aligned suppression. Aggregate only, DUA required for research use.
specification.reform_value: Safety-first metric. Quantifies a previously invisible harm pathway.

metrics.name: Cost to Taxpayers Per Case
metrics.description: Estimated public cost per family court case, including judicial time and indigent services.

specification.definition: Estimated per-case public expenditure including prorated judicial time, clerical time, court-appointed GAL fees paid from public funds, indigent representation, and facility overhead.
specification.numerator: Total annual family court operational cost
specification.source: State court budget, OSCA time studies, GAL payment records
specification.inclusion: All family court cases statewide
specification.exclusion: Privately-paid services
specification.confounders: Allocation methodology differences, indirect cost assumptions
specification.min_sample: State-level always publishable
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Grounds legislative debate in fiscal reality. Supports ROI analysis of reform investments.

metrics.name: Longitudinal Case Data Set
metrics.description: De-identified case-level data with linked outcomes over five+ years. DUA required.

specification.definition: De-identified case-level records including party attributes, case characteristics, rulings, and longitudinal outcomes (re-filings, enforcement actions) over a rolling 5-year window.
specification.numerator: Not a rate; a dataset
specification.source: Court case management system with longitudinal linkage
specification.inclusion: All cases within the window with k-anonymity ≥ 10 in relevant groups
specification.exclusion: Sealed cases, cases involving parties opting out of research use
specification.confounders: De-identification noise, missing outcome linkages
specification.min_sample: Individual research cohorts require IRB approval specifying minimum size
specification.privacy: Tier B. DUA required. IRB approval required. Re-identification prohibited with permanent revocation as penalty.
specification.reform_value: Enables rigorous academic research. Foundation for disparity studies, intervention efficacy research, and parenting plan durability analysis.

metrics.name: Demographic Outcome Differentials
metrics.description: Case outcomes by protected class, controlled for case mix. Disparity study foundation.

specification.definition: Analysis of family court outcome differentials by demographic characteristics (income, race, ethnicity, representation status) controlling for case complexity.
specification.numerator: Outcome values within each demographic group
specification.denominator: Case count within each demographic group
specification.source: Court case management system with demographic coding (subject to availability), linked to ACS for contextual variables
specification.inclusion: Cases with sufficient demographic coding completeness
specification.exclusion: Cases with ambiguous demographic classification
specification.confounders: Demographic coding practice differences, self-reported vs observed classifications
specification.min_sample: Each demographic cohort requires n ≥ 100 for statistical validity
specification.privacy: Tier B. DUA required. Demographic breakdowns published only with k-anonymity ≥ 10 and disparity context.
specification.reform_value: Foundational disparity research capability. Informs targeted reform investments and evaluates existing policies.

metrics.name: Parenting Plan Effectiveness
metrics.description: Linked longitudinal data: plan type → stability outcomes → return-to-court.

specification.definition: Causal-inference-ready dataset linking parenting plan type, compliance data (where available), and longitudinal outcomes including return-to-court, enforcement actions, and child welfare system involvement.
specification.numerator: Not a rate; a research dataset
specification.denominator: Not a rate; a research dataset
specification.source: Court case management + CoTrackPro compliance data + child welfare cross-links (state agency MOU)
specification.inclusion: Cases with complete plan coding and at least 24 months of outcome follow-up
specification.exclusion: Cases with sealed follow-up or interstate transfer
specification.confounders: Self-selection into plan types, unmeasured child and family factors
specification.min_sample: Research cohorts require n ≥ 500 for adequate statistical power
specification.privacy: Tier B. DUA required. IRB approval required. Cross-agency linkages require additional data sharing agreements.
specification.reform_value: Enables evidence-based reform of parenting plan presumptions and interventions.

metrics.name: Intervention Efficacy Data
metrics.description: Outcomes of court-ordered interventions — counseling, mediation, parenting classes.

specification.definition: Outcomes data for court-ordered interventions, linking intervention type and completion to longitudinal case outcomes.
specification.numerator: Outcome variable values
specification.source: Court orders, intervention provider completion records, longitudinal case outcomes
specification.inclusion: Cases with court-ordered intervention and completion documentation
specification.exclusion: Cases with disputed completion, voluntary self-selection into interventions
specification.confounders: Self-selection, provider quality variance, completion definition differences
specification.min_sample: Each intervention type requires n ≥ 100
specification.privacy: Tier B. DUA required.
specification.reform_value: Tests whether specific court interventions actually improve outcomes. Informs evidence-based intervention orders.

metrics.name: Cross-State Comparative Dataset
metrics.description: Standardized schema enabling cross-jurisdiction research.

specification.definition: Schema-harmonized dataset combining Missouri data with participating peer states, enabling cross-jurisdiction comparative research.
specification.numerator: Not a rate; a dataset
specification.source: Participating state court data under multi-state MOU
specification.inclusion: States that have adopted the Metric Catalog schema
specification.exclusion: Non-conforming data fields; suppressed cells
specification.confounders: Schema drift, coding practice differences across states
specification.min_sample: Each participating state contributes only fields meeting its own suppression thresholds
specification.privacy: Tier B. Multi-state DUA required. IRB approval required.
specification.reform_value: Enables comparative research that reveals which state-level policy choices produce better outcomes.

metrics.name: County Performance Dashboard
metrics.description: Aggregate case flow, disposition, and outcome distribution by county.

specification.definition: Public dashboard with all Tier A metrics broken out by county, with methodology links, confidence intervals, and comparison to state average.
specification.numerator: Varies by metric
specification.source: Court case management system via OSCA
specification.inclusion: All counties meeting minimum sample thresholds
specification.exclusion: Counties below n thresholds suppressed
specification.confounders: Vary by underlying metric
specification.min_sample: Each metric applies its own minimum
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Enables data-driven reporting on family court performance. Reduces reliance on anecdote.

metrics.name: Trend Anomaly Alerts
metrics.description: Statistically significant deviations in metrics by jurisdiction, flagged monthly.

specification.definition: Automated flagging of statistically significant month-over-month or year-over-year changes in jurisdiction-level metrics, with methodology disclosed.
specification.numerator: Actual metric value minus expected
specification.source: Metric Catalog data pipeline
specification.inclusion: Metrics with sufficient baseline history
specification.exclusion: Metrics newly introduced with insufficient baseline
specification.confounders: Seasonality, one-time events, reporting changes
specification.min_sample: Baseline of 24 months required
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Supports investigative journalism. Surfaces potential issues for administrative review.

metrics.name: FOIA Response Time Tracker
metrics.description: How long court administrators take to respond to records requests.

specification.definition: Aggregate elapsed days from Sunshine Law request submission to substantive response (release, partial release with exemption citation, or denial) by the responding circuit.
specification.numerator: Days from submission to response
specification.source: Voluntary Sunshine Law request logs, public records requestor surveys
specification.inclusion: Requests submitted under Chapter 610 RSMo
specification.exclusion: Requests voluntarily withdrawn, commercial records-service requests
specification.confounders: Request complexity, requester responsiveness to clarification, volume of concurrent requests
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 20 requests per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Accountability metric for Sunshine Law compliance. Supports press access.

metrics.name: Judicial Vacancy & Load
metrics.description: Open judicial positions and caseload-per-judge by circuit.

specification.definition: Current judicial vacancies and average caseload per active judge, by circuit.
specification.numerator: Active cases at snapshot
specification.denominator: Active judges assigned to the case type
specification.source: OSCA judicial roster; court case management system
specification.inclusion: All circuits
specification.exclusion: Circuits mid-boundary-realignment
specification.confounders: Circuit sharing arrangements, senior judge participation
specification.min_sample: All circuits
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Informs judicial appointment and funding discussions.

metrics.name: Methodology Audit Log
metrics.description: Public record of every change to metric definitions — essential for credibility.

specification.definition: Public immutable log of every methodology change, including date, version, rationale, board vote, and comment period summary.
specification.numerator: Not a rate; a log
specification.source: Methodology Board proceedings, version control system
specification.inclusion: Every methodology version change
specification.exclusion: None
specification.confounders: None
specification.min_sample: Not applicable
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Essential credibility infrastructure. Allows reproducibility across methodology revisions.

metrics.name: Protective Order Grant Rate
metrics.description: Aggregate grant rate for protective orders, with confidence intervals.

specification.definition: Proportion of filed petitions for adult abuse protective orders (ex parte and full) that are granted, with confidence intervals, at circuit level.
specification.numerator: Petitions granted in full or in part
specification.denominator: Petitions adjudicated (granted, denied, or dismissed on merits)
specification.source: Court case management system, Chapter 455 RSMo records
specification.inclusion: Adult abuse petitions
specification.exclusion: Child abuse petitions (separate reporting), cases transferred to criminal court
specification.confounders: Petition quality, representation, local procedural differences
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 25 petitions per quarter per § 455.085 suppression threshold
specification.privacy: Suppression threshold of 25 petitions per reporting period per county (see Protective Order Data Confidentiality).
specification.reform_value: Addresses long-standing public and advocacy interest in grant-rate variation while protecting survivor safety.

metrics.name: Bilateral Violation Tracking
metrics.description: Compliance violations measured in both directions — essential for neutrality.

specification.definition: Compliance violations logged and verified, measured separately in both directions (Parent A→B and Parent B→A), with outcome tracking.
specification.numerator: Verified violations by each parent
specification.denominator: Total documented exchanges or communication opportunities
specification.source: User-logged exchanges, time-stamped evidence, court filings where disputes arise
specification.inclusion: Cases with bilateral logging enabled; both parents using compatible tooling
specification.exclusion: Cases with court-ordered no-contact in one direction
specification.confounders: Differential logging adherence, cultural framing of violations
specification.min_sample: n ≥ 20 exchange events per direction before rate is calculated
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time. Aggregates stripped of identifiers; gender and case-type stratification only above cell thresholds.
specification.reform_value: Ends the one-sided metric problem. Any measurement in only one direction immediately discredits the framework.

metrics.name: Representation Disparity
metrics.description: Counsel access rates by case type, income, and location.

specification.definition: Proportion of cases where each party has legal representation, stratified by case type and by available proxy income indicators (poverty rate of residential zip code).
specification.numerator: Cases with at least one represented party
specification.source: Court representation records, ACS data for zip-level income proxy
specification.inclusion: All family court filings
specification.exclusion: Cases with PO Box or missing residential address
specification.confounders: Zip-level income is a proxy; individual income not coded
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 100
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Foundational access-to-justice metric. Supports legal aid resource allocation.

metrics.name: Child Voice Inclusion Rate
metrics.description: Proportion of cases where a child interview or preference is documented.

specification.definition: Proportion of contested custody cases with at least one documented method of capturing the child's preference (in-camera interview, GAL interview, custody evaluator, age-appropriate survey).
specification.numerator: Cases with documented child-voice capture
specification.denominator: Contested custody cases with child(ren) age 6+
specification.source: Court records, GAL reports, case file review sampling
specification.inclusion: Contested custody cases with at least one child age 6 or older
specification.exclusion: Cases where child voice inclusion is contraindicated (documented abuse, mental-health contraindication)
specification.confounders: Documentation practice differences, local norms
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 30
specification.privacy: Tier B with DUA.
specification.reform_value: Surfaces whether children's voices are being heard in decisions about their lives.

metrics.name: Resource Referral Follow-Through
metrics.description: User-reported completion rates for court-ordered services and resources.

specification.definition: User-reported completion rates for court-ordered services and resources (parenting classes, counseling, substance treatment, mediation), measured from opt-in user base.
specification.numerator: User-reported completions
specification.source: CoTrackPro opt-in user reports
specification.inclusion: Users who have opted into research participation
specification.exclusion: Non-participating users
specification.confounders: Self-report bias, selection bias in participating users
specification.min_sample: n ≥ 50 reports per referral category
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Reveals whether ordered services actually happen. Informs court resource partnerships.

metrics.name: Routine Stability Indicator
metrics.description: Consistency of bedtime, meals, homework, school across households.

specification.definition: Aggregate index of routine consistency across a child's two households, based on opted-in parent and age-appropriate child logging of bedtime, meal patterns, homework routines, and school-day structure.
specification.numerator: Routine events aligned across households
specification.source: Opt-in parent and child logging
specification.inclusion: Families with opt-in from both parents and age-appropriate child participation
specification.exclusion: Families with active safety concerns; children below age 6
specification.confounders: Differential logging practices, divergent family philosophies about routines
specification.min_sample: Individual scores suppressed; only cohort aggregates published
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time. Child-level data never published. Cohort aggregates only, with parental consent.
specification.reform_value: Surfaces whether two-household arrangements maintain the consistency children need for healthy development.

metrics.name: School Continuity
metrics.description: Changes in school, attendance, and tardiness correlated with exchange patterns.

specification.definition: School enrollment changes, attendance rates, and tardiness rates for children in family court cases, correlated with custody exchange patterns.
specification.numerator: Varies by sub-metric
specification.source: Opt-in parent reports, with FERPA-compliant school data linkage where parent consent provided
specification.inclusion: Children age 5-17 in opt-in families
specification.exclusion: Home-schooled children (separate analysis), private schools without data-sharing
specification.confounders: Family moves unrelated to custody, school district policies, school quality differences
specification.min_sample: Circuit-level requires n ≥ 50 enrolled children
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time. FERPA-compliant. School data accessed only with explicit parental consent.
specification.reform_value: Identifies stability impacts of custody arrangements on the most measurable aspect of child life.

metrics.name: Activity Continuity
metrics.description: Sports, therapy, and medical appointments maintained across both households.

specification.definition: Proportion of a child's ongoing activities, therapy appointments, and medical care that are maintained consistently across both households.
specification.numerator: Activities maintained with both parents' participation or facilitation
specification.source: Opt-in parent logging
specification.inclusion: Families with opt-in from both parents
specification.exclusion: Activities newly introduced by one parent
specification.confounders: Differential parent tracking, legitimate disagreements about activity appropriateness
specification.min_sample: Cohort-level requires n ≥ 50 families
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Detects when family court arrangements fragment the child's life. A humane, measurable impact signal.

metrics.name: Sibling & Extended Family Contact
metrics.description: Frequency of contact with siblings and extended family. A wellbeing proxy.

specification.definition: Frequency of documented contact between a child and their siblings (including half- and step-siblings) and extended family members (grandparents, aunts, uncles).
specification.numerator: Documented contact events
specification.source: Opt-in parent and age-appropriate child logging
specification.inclusion: Families with at least one sibling or documented extended family relationship
specification.exclusion: Families where contact is legally restricted
specification.confounders: Geographic distance, family conflict with extended relatives, pre-existing relationship quality
specification.min_sample: Cohort-level requires n ≥ 50
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Extended family ties are a protective factor in child development. Measurement makes maintenance visible.

metrics.name: Child Wellbeing Self-Report
metrics.description: Opt-in, age-appropriate wellbeing reporting. Never disclosed individually.

specification.definition: Age-appropriate, opt-in child wellbeing reporting using validated short-form instruments (e.g., KINDL-R for children 7+).
specification.numerator: Wellbeing score
specification.source: Validated child self-report instruments administered through opt-in platform
specification.inclusion: Children age 7+ with parental opt-in and age-appropriate assent
specification.exclusion: Children with documented distress responses to prior administration
specification.confounders: Instrument limitations, transient mood effects, caregiver proximity during administration
specification.min_sample: Individual scores never published. Only aggregates of n ≥ 100 children released.
specification.privacy: Tier D. Individual responses never disclosed, never used in any court proceeding, never linked back to the responding child's case. Aggregate-only release, with periodic data destruction.
specification.reform_value: Ultimate outcome metric. Answers the question that matters most: are the children okay?

metrics.name: Conflict Intensity Trajectory
metrics.description: NLP-scored hostility trend from co-parent communication. Transparent methodology.

specification.definition: Hostility intensity score (0-100) derived from NLP analysis of opted-in co-parent communication, using a transparent, peer-reviewed linguistic feature model.
specification.numerator: Weighted hostility features in messages (negation, second-person blame, intensifiers, catastrophizing, absolutism)
specification.source: User-opted-in CoTrackPro communication archive
specification.inclusion: Parents who have explicitly opted into research participation with active communication
specification.exclusion: One-way communication only, legal hold communications, attorney-inclusive threads
specification.confounders: Cultural differences in directness, language non-native effects, emergency communications, documented crises
specification.min_sample: Individual score suppressed; only cohort-level aggregates published (county, case type, tenure)
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time. Individual message content never leaves user account. Only scored features aggregated with DP noise.
specification.reform_value: First large-scale measurement of whether de-escalation interventions actually work.

metrics.name: De-escalation Tool Usage
metrics.description: Frequency of rewrite-before-send and similar tools. Adoption signal.

specification.definition: Rate at which users invoke rewrite-before-send and similar de-escalation tools, and the rate at which suggested revisions are adopted.
specification.numerator: Tool invocations with adoption of suggested revision
specification.source: Platform analytics from opted-in users
specification.inclusion: Users with opt-in to anonymous usage analytics
specification.exclusion: Non-participating users
specification.confounders: Tool design changes, user familiarity growth
specification.min_sample: Cohort-level requires n ≥ 100 users
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Adoption signal for de-escalation technology. Informs product and intervention design.

metrics.name: Repair Attempt Rate
metrics.description: Messages acknowledging prior conflict constructively — a resilience indicator.

specification.definition: Proportion of messages in conflict-escalated threads that meet the definition of a repair attempt (acknowledgment, apology, reframing, or constructive redirection).
specification.numerator: Messages coded as repair attempts
specification.source: Opt-in communication archive with NLP coding
specification.inclusion: Threads in which hostility score exceeded a threshold
specification.exclusion: Ambiguous or contested coding
specification.confounders: Cultural and linguistic variation in repair expression
specification.min_sample: Cohort-level requires n ≥ 100 threads
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: Resilience measurement. Identifies whether relationships are capable of recovery.

metrics.name: Critical Incident Flags
metrics.description: Aggregated, anonymized critical incidents. Safety-signal reporting.

specification.definition: Aggregated count of critical incidents (suicide mention, threat, allegation of harm to child, allegation of weapon) flagged by users or automated detection in opt-in communications.
specification.numerator: Flagged incidents in period
specification.source: User flags plus automated content screening on opt-in data
specification.inclusion: Opt-in users
specification.exclusion: False-positive flags after human review
specification.confounders: Detection sensitivity settings, false positive rates
specification.min_sample: Aggregate-only reporting; never individual
specification.privacy: Tier B with DUA. Never individual-level. Mandatory reporting to appropriate safety authorities when thresholds are met regardless of aggregate reporting.
specification.reform_value: Safety signal aggregation. Identifies concerning patterns at jurisdiction level.

metrics.name: Service Utilization Trends
metrics.description: Use of mental health services pre/during/post case, at population level.

specification.definition: Aggregate rates of mental health service utilization among adults and children involved in family court proceedings, at population level.
specification.numerator: Service utilization events
specification.source: Medicaid claims, private insurance aggregate reporting, state mental health agency data — all via DUA where required
specification.inclusion: Adults and minors with family court case in the period
specification.exclusion: Records without reliable family court linkage
specification.confounders: Insurance coverage variance, service availability, stigma
specification.min_sample: State-level aggregate only
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline. HIPAA-compliant aggregate-only reporting.
specification.reform_value: Quantifies mental health impact of family court involvement at population level. Informs service investment.

metrics.name: Active Order Status Check
metrics.description: Real-time existence and terms of active custody or protective orders. Already partially available.

specification.definition: Real-time API for officers responding to calls to verify existence, terms, and contact restrictions of active family court orders (custody, protective).
specification.numerator: Not a rate; a lookup service
specification.denominator: Not a rate; a lookup service
specification.source: State court case management system; integration with existing law enforcement data networks
specification.inclusion: All active family court orders with enforcement relevance
specification.exclusion: Sealed orders, orders outside enforcement scope
specification.confounders: Data freshness lag (target < 15 minutes)
specification.min_sample: Service-level; no statistical sampling
specification.privacy: Tier A for officer access with audit log. Aggregate usage statistics Tier A.
specification.reform_value: Reduces cross-jurisdiction enforcement failures. Already partially available; this extends and standardizes.

metrics.name: Violation Report Turnaround
metrics.description: Time from officer-documented violation to court action.

specification.definition: Elapsed days from officer-documented violation report submission to first court action (hearing scheduled, summons issued, or disposition).
specification.numerator: Days from report to action
specification.source: Integrated law enforcement and court systems
specification.inclusion: Formally filed violation reports
specification.exclusion: Informal referrals, declined prosecutions without court filing
specification.confounders: Jurisdictional workflow differences, prosecution filing practices
specification.min_sample: Jurisdiction requires n ≥ 20 per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Quantifies enforcement pipeline efficiency. Identifies bottlenecks.

metrics.name: Custody Exchange Call Volume
metrics.description: Dispatch calls tied to custody exchange points. Hotspot mapping.

specification.definition: Count of dispatch calls at documented custody exchange locations (e.g., police department lobbies, designated safe exchange sites), mapped to geographic hotspots.
specification.numerator: Dispatch calls at or near exchange locations
specification.source: Law enforcement dispatch records with location coding
specification.inclusion: Calls coded as custody-exchange-related
specification.exclusion: Calls at same location unrelated to custody
specification.confounders: Coding practice differences, location overlap
specification.min_sample: Hotspot analysis requires n ≥ 25 calls per location per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline. Address-level data suppressed; presented only as heat maps.
specification.reform_value: Identifies where exchange arrangements are failing. Supports safer-exchange infrastructure investment.

metrics.name: Cross-Jurisdiction Order Verification
metrics.description: Confirmed enforceability of out-of-state orders. A gap in current systems.

specification.definition: Success rate for real-time verification of out-of-state family court orders presented to responding officers.
specification.numerator: Verified orders
specification.source: Law enforcement verification system logs
specification.inclusion: Verification attempts during officer response
specification.exclusion: Non-urgent verification requests
specification.confounders: Other-state system availability, information completeness at point of request
specification.min_sample: Jurisdiction-level requires n ≥ 20 per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Closes interstate enforcement gap. Supports FFVE (Full Faith and Credit for Protection Orders Act) implementation.

metrics.name: Settlement Rate
metrics.description: Proportion of referred cases reaching full or partial agreement.

specification.definition: Proportion of cases referred to mediation that reach full or partial agreement within the mediation process.
specification.numerator: Cases with full or partial agreement
specification.source: Mediation program records
specification.inclusion: Cases formally referred and entering mediation
specification.exclusion: Cases withdrawn before initial mediation session
specification.confounders: Referral criteria differences across programs, mediator experience variance
specification.min_sample: Program-level requires n ≥ 30 per year
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Standard program outcome metric.

metrics.name: Durability of Agreements
metrics.description: Proportion of mediated agreements that hold without return-to-court within 24 months.

specification.definition: Proportion of cases reaching mediated agreement that remain stable (no return-to-court on the same substantive issues) for 24 months post-agreement.
specification.numerator: Mediated-agreement cases stable at 24 months
specification.denominator: Total mediated-agreement cases with 24+ months of follow-up
specification.source: Mediation program records plus court case management system
specification.inclusion: Cases with documented mediation agreement
specification.exclusion: Cases transferred to other jurisdictions, cases with agreements later vacated on appeal
specification.confounders: Cases most likely to succeed may be pre-selected to mediation; agreement quality variance
specification.min_sample: Program-level requires n ≥ 30 agreements with 24-month follow-up
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: True success metric for mediation programs. Distinguishes genuine resolution from pyrrhic agreement.

metrics.name: Session Count to Resolution
metrics.description: Average sessions needed, by case complexity.

specification.definition: Mean and median number of mediation sessions required to reach resolution (agreement, partial agreement, or termination without agreement), stratified by case complexity.
specification.numerator: Sum of sessions across cases
specification.source: Mediation program records
specification.inclusion: Cases reaching any terminal status
specification.exclusion: Ongoing cases
specification.confounders: Case complexity, mediator style, party cooperation
specification.min_sample: Program-level requires n ≥ 30
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Efficiency measure. Supports program sizing and budget decisions.

metrics.name: Post-Mediation Conflict Intensity
metrics.description: Measured reduction in communication hostility post-mediation. Efficacy signal.

specification.definition: Change in hostility-intensity score (see Conflict Intensity Trajectory) from 30 days pre-mediation to 90 days post-mediation, for opted-in participants.
specification.numerator: Post-mediation score minus pre-mediation score
specification.source: Opt-in CoTrackPro communication data
specification.inclusion: Participants with pre- and post-mediation communication data
specification.exclusion: Participants with communication suspension during window
specification.confounders: Regression to mean, other concurrent interventions
specification.min_sample: Cohort-level requires n ≥ 50 matched cases
specification.privacy: Raw data stays in the user account. Only aggregates leave the user environment and only after explicit opt-in. User can withdraw at any time.
specification.reform_value: First rigorous signal of whether mediation reduces sustained conflict. Major research contribution.

metrics.name: Workforce Impact Estimate
metrics.description: Aggregate estimate of workforce hours lost to family court attendance.

specification.definition: State-level estimate of workforce hours lost to court attendance, derived from average-hearing-per-case figures × family court case volume.
specification.numerator: Estimated hours of court-attendance-related work loss
specification.source: Court case management system × OSCA hearing-time studies × Bureau of Labor Statistics workforce data
specification.inclusion: All Missouri family court cases involving working-age parties
specification.exclusion: Cases where parties are unemployed or retired
specification.confounders: Work-from-home and flexible-schedule prevalence, court calendar practices
specification.min_sample: State-level always publishable
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Quantifies family court as a workforce wellness issue. Engages employer constituency.

metrics.name: EAP Family Law Referral Volume
metrics.description: Requests for family-law-related EAP services, anonymized.

specification.definition: Aggregate count and rate of family-law-related referrals to Employee Assistance Programs, reported by participating EAP providers.
specification.numerator: Family-law-related referrals
specification.source: Participating EAP provider aggregate reports
specification.inclusion: Referrals categorized as family/relationship/legal
specification.exclusion: Non-participating EAP providers
specification.confounders: Provider participation coverage, categorization practice differences
specification.min_sample: Statewide aggregate requires n ≥ 1,000 EAP utilizations per quarter in participating providers
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Employer-facing workforce wellness metric. Supports employer engagement in reform.

metrics.name: Court-Related Leave Patterns
metrics.description: Aggregate leave tied to custody exchanges and court dates.

specification.definition: Estimated aggregate leave usage (PTO, sick leave, FMLA where applicable) tied to family court obligations, derived from employer survey data and court scheduling records.
specification.numerator: Estimated leave hours
specification.source: Employer survey data plus OSCA court scheduling data
specification.inclusion: Missouri-employed parties to family court cases
specification.exclusion: Self-employed, federal employees
specification.confounders: Survey response bias, employer leave policy variance
specification.min_sample: State-level with at least 50 participating employers
specification.privacy: Aggregate only. k-anonymity threshold of 10. Differential privacy noise applied to small cells. No individual or case identifier ever leaves the pipeline.
specification.reform_value: Quantifies private-sector economic impact. Supports reform coalition building with business community.

_notes: This file is generated from scripts/build/_data.js. Do not edit directly.
Methodology changes require the Adoption Workflow (docs/02-governance/09-metric-adoption-workflow.docx).
Coercive-control metrics from the mini-binder (docs/07-coercive-control/cc-04-metric-additions.docx) are NOT included in this export until the Expert Review Convening validates them.
